# [enhancement] Promote GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD to aiConfig.data for human tuning

## Context

PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) (resolves #10035) introduced `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.8` as a named constant inside `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine.mjs` with documented derivation from the `ConceptService` weight formula. Deferred config-lifting to this follow-up ticket to keep the refactor's scope focused on the architectural change (regex + LLM â†’ graph traversal).

The threshold governs when a concept lacking an `EXPLAINED_BY` edge emits a `[GUIDE_GAP]`. Default value `0.8` means "at least tier-1 baseline priority" â€” every tier-1 concept without a guide qualifies; tier-2/3 concepts qualify only if uniqueness + coverage-deficit bump them above. The derivation is stable, but future ontology growth (post-#10050/#10036/#10037) may reveal the default needs tuning without triggering a code change.

## The Fix

1. Promote the constant to `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`
2. Preserve the default value (`0.8`)
3. Preserve the derivation comment â€” move it from the constant's JSDoc to the config-template comment so curators see the rationale when tuning
4. Import + consume the config value in `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps`
5. Run `node ./buildScripts/ai/initServerConfigs.mjs` will pick up the new config key on install â€” verify no regeneration needed on existing installs (should be fine since it's additive)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` exists in `config.template.mjs` with default `0.8` + derivation comment
- [ ] `GapInferenceEngine` reads the value from config, not from a file-local constant
- [ ] Existing PR #10084 tests still pass (regression guard)
- [ ] New test: threshold override via config produces different gap-emission behavior

`62d6f155-e57f-4279-9b59-36c9e4ecbc5e` â€” flagged as out-of-scope in PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) body

- Weight formula: `ConceptService.calculateWeight` â€” `tier_score (0.8/0.5/0.3) + uniqueness (0.2) + coverage_deficit (0.3)`

- 2026-04-19T15:19:00Z @tobiu referenced in commit `db5bdae` - "feat(ai): config-lift guideGapWeightThreshold to aiConfig.data (#10086)

Promotes the previously file-local `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.8` constant
in `GapInferenceEngine` to `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold`, making the
weight gate curator-tunable without a code change. Default value preserved
(`0.8`); the derivation comment moves from the const JSDoc to the config
template next to the value itself, where tuners will see it.

## Scope

- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`: adds `guideGapWeightThreshold:
  Number(process.env.NEO_GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD) || 0.8` after `decayFactor`
  (both graph-pipeline behavior floats â€” semantic grouping). JSDoc carries the
  full derivation from ConceptService.calculateWeight plus the post-#10087
  context (threshold now gates three signals, not just GUIDE_GAP â€” historical
  prefix retained per ticket AC).
- `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine.mjs`: removes the file-local const,
  imports `Memory_Config as aiConfig` via the services SDK, caches the
  threshold once per cycle at the top of `inferConceptGraphGaps` so the live
  config value is consulted (tests can mutate it; module-load capture would
  miss runtime overrides). Class + method JSDoc updated to reference the
  config path and explain the historical `guideGap*` prefix (introduced in
  #10035 for GUIDE_GAP alone; widened in #10087 to gate all three concept-
  graph signals â€” rename considered but scoped out to keep this PR tight).

**Parity note:** the gitignored local `config.mjs` must mirror the template
addition for tests to pick up the key. Updated locally in this worktree but
not committed (by design â€” the template is the source of truth for the
shipped repo; each checkout's `config.mjs` is derived manually or via
`bootstrapWorktree.mjs` on worktree creation).

## Tests (41/41 stable Ă— 5 runs)

New test `threshold override via aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold
changes emission behavior (#10086)`:
- Plants a tier-2 concept with weight 0.5 (below default 0.8 threshold).
- Overrides `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold = 0.3` inside try/finally.
- Asserts the concept now emits both `[GUIDE_GAP]` and `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]`,
  proving the config is read at gate time (not captured at module load).
- Restores the original value in `finally`.

The existing "skip low-weight concepts" test (weight 0.3 with default 0.8)
remains and now acts as the counterpart â€” default config path.

## AC checklist

- [x] `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` exists in `config.template.mjs`
      with default 0.8 + derivation comment (moved from GapInferenceEngine)
- [x] `GapInferenceEngine` reads the value from config, not from a file-local
      constant â€” verified via runtime-override test
- [x] Existing tests pass (40 â†’ 41 total, all green Ă— 5 consecutive runs)
- [x] New test proves threshold override via config produces different
      gap-emission behavior

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T15:20:04Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10102
- 2026-04-19T15:31:57Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10103
- 2026-04-19T15:33:22Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-04-19T15:39:36Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10104
- 2026-04-19T16:05:15Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10105
- 2026-04-19T16:26:28Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4df4b09` - "feat(ai): config-lift ConceptDiscoveryService tuning knobs (#10036)

Addresses tobi's polish catch on PR #10105: `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` and
`MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH = 200` were file-local magic numbers. Operator-tuning knobs
belong in config, not as consts buried in the service. Same discipline as
#10086 (`guideGapWeightThreshold` config-lift).

## Scope

- `config.template.mjs` + local `config.mjs`: new `conceptDiscovery` nested
  object grouping `prScanLimit` (default 20) + `minSourceLength` (default 200).
  Env overrides via `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_PR_SCAN_LIMIT` +
  `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH`. Nested grouping matches existing
  `openAiCompatible` / `ollama` convention for service-scoped configs.
- `ConceptDiscoveryService.mjs`: dropped the two constants. Reads from
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.prScanLimit ?? 20` +
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.minSourceLength ?? 200` at call time (live
  reads, not module-load capture â€” enables runtime tuning + test overrides
  without reimport). Instance-level `prScanLimit` field retained for test
  overrides (falls through to aiConfig when null).
- Parity: local `config.mjs` mirrors the template addition (gitignored per
  `.gitignore`; not committed â€” only template goes into git).

## Out of scope (tracked for #10103)

Per `feedback_config_service_boundary.md` + #10103: these keys belong on the
SDK-layer config (ConceptDiscoveryService is a daemon service, not a memory-
core concern). Placed in memory-core config as the pragmatic destination today
â€” same debt as `guideGapWeightThreshold`. Migration is #10103's scope.

## Tests (49/49 stable Ă— 3 runs)

New test: `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery.minSourceLength` override changes
extraction behavior. Proves the config is read at call time (raise threshold
above body length â†’ LLM skipped; restore default â†’ LLM invoked).

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T16:30:19Z @tobiu referenced in commit `a5802d7` - "feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService + validated flag for mined candidates (#10036) (#10105)

* feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService (LLM-based) + validated flag (#10036)

Lands deliverables 1, 2, and 4 of #10036 in one cohesive slice per the intake
discussion.

## Approach

LLM-driven concept extraction via `OpenAiCompatible.generate` (gemma4 or
configured provider â€” same substrate used by `SemanticGraphExtractor` and
`GoldenPathSynthesizer`). Two high-signal corpora:

- **Epics** â€” issue markdown files labeled `epic` in `resources/content/issues/`.
  Epic bodies are curated distilled architecture.
- **Pull requests** â€” `resources/content/pulls/*.md`, including their
  `## Comments` section. PR review comments contain `[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` /
  `[RETROSPECTIVE]` / `[KB_GAP]` / "Gold Standards" / "Traps Avoided" prose â€”
  the exact architectural discourse where new vocabulary emerges. Sorted
  descending by PR number (freshest discourse first), capped at
  `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` per cycle to bound LLM cost.

The system prompt encodes the Teaching Test + explicit anti-patterns (class
names, file paths, framework names without architectural weight, routine
ticket vocabulary). Output is strict JSON parsed via `Json.extract` for
fence-tolerance. Per-source malformed output is logged and skipped â€” never
fatal to the cycle.

## Schema (Deliverable 4)

- `validated: boolean` field on concept nodes. Default `undefined` (legacy)
  treated as validated (no data migration). Mined candidates carry
  `validated: false`, tier 3, default weight â†’ silenced in sandman_handoff.md
  via two independent mechanisms (weight gate + validated gate).
- `ConceptIngestor.computePayloadHash` includes validated, so flipping
  curator-review state triggers re-upsert.
- `ConceptIngestor` upsert-properties include validated, propagating to graph.
- `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps` skips `validated === false`
  concepts regardless of weight â€” belt-and-suspenders alongside the weight
  gate. All three signals (GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP / ORPHAN_CONCEPT) respect
  the gate.
- Curator promotion = edit nodes.jsonl: flip `validated: true` + bump tier
  and/or weight. Git diff is the review surface.

## Tests (48/48 stable Ă— 5 runs)

- `ConceptDiscoveryService.spec.mjs` (6 tests): epic-label filtering,
  PR cap + recency ordering, ConceptService dedupe (by id + alias),
  malformed-LLM tolerance, MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH short-circuit, end-to-end
  runDiscoveryCycle + JSONL append. Mocks `OpenAiCompatible.prototype.generate`
  with queued responses per call. Symmetric afterEach per
  `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs` new test: `validated: false` silences all three
  concept-graph signals even with weight 1.3 > 0.8 threshold.

## Deferred (out of this PR)

- **Deliverable 3** (KB-assisted classification) â€” blocked on KB MCP timeout
  per ticket body.
- **Deliverable 5** (glossary gap report) â€” pure reporting tool; file when
  ontology matures.
- **REM-cycle integration** â€” manual invocation via
  `DreamService.runConceptDiscovery()` for now. Per-cycle LLM cost would
  flood nodes.jsonl; deliberate to let curator/operator set cadence.
- **Session-summary mining** â€” epics + PRs are higher signal; add
  session-based mining as a follow-up if epic/PR coverage proves insufficient.
